title: Resume Screening AI System - Trustworthiness Evaluation

problem_domain.description: Automated system that ranks resumes to identify best candidates for E-commerce Specialist roles
problem_domain.value_proposition: Reduce hiring bias, improve hiring speed, identify overlooked talent

stakeholders_harmed.job_seekers: False negatives: Qualified candidates eliminated at early stages
stakeholders_harmed.hiring_managers: False positives: Recommends unqualified candidates, wasting time
stakeholders_harmed.companies: Bad hires from AI-recommended but unqualified candidates
stakeholders_harmed.diversity: Algorithm may perpetuate existing bias in hiring data

requirements: Must flag uncertainty when methods disagree strongly
Cannot eliminate qualified candidates without human review
False negative rate < 20% (don't miss qualified talent)

metric_1.name: Top-K Stability Score (TSS)
metric_1.definition: Overlap of top-K recommendations between different ranking methods

interpretation.< 0.4: Low trust - methods strongly disagree (hallucination risk?)

metric_1.justification: When TF-IDF and embeddings rank same candidates similarly, we have higher confidence. Disagreement suggests one method may be hallucinating or overfitting.

metric_2.name: Accuracy vs Ground Truth
metric_2.definition: Percentage of top-K recommendations that are actually qualified (marked 'select')
metric_2.justification: True proxy for trustworthiness. If system recommends unqualified people, hiring managers will distrust it.

metric_3.name: Hallucination Detection Rate
metric_3.definition: Percentage of LLM-claimed skills not found in resume text
metric_3.justification: LLM hallucinations undermine trust. If system claims candidate has SQL skills but resume doesn't mention it, hiring manager catches the lie.

metric_4.name: Method Agreement (Correlation)
metric_4.definition: Correlation of scores between TF-IDF and embeddings
metric_4.justification: When keyword and semantic methods agree, we're more confident. Disagreement = potential issue.

case_1.name: LLM Hallucination: Claimed Skills Not in Resume
case_1.description: LLM mentions required skills (e.g., 'SQL', 'AWS') that don't appear anywhere in the resume text
case_1.method: text matching: check if claimed skill appears in resume[lower()]
case_1.actual_result: NOT OBSERVED in sample - LLM used mock scoring, no actual hallucinations detected
case_1.severity: CRITICAL - Could cause hiring of unqualified candidates

case_2.name: Method Disagreement: TSS < 0.4
case_2.description: TF-IDF and embeddings rank top-5 completely differently, indicating possible hallucination or keyword-semantic mismatch

case_3.name: Poor Accuracy: < 40% of Top-K are Actually Selected
case_3.description: System recommends unqualified candidates. More than 60% of top recommendations are marked 'reject' in ground truth
case_3.severity: CRITICAL - False positives waste hiring manager time

case_4.name: Insufficient Data Diversity
case_4.description: Dataset is too homogeneous or imbalanced (select rate < 20% or > 80%)

case_5.name: Ensemble Ensemble Regression
case_5.description: Ensemble method performs WORSE than best single method
case_5.severity: HIGH - Ensemble failed to improve trustworthiness

modification_proposed.name: Selective Ensemble with Confidence Scoring
modification_proposed.description: Instead of simple averaging, use confidence scores to weight methods. When methods disagree strongly (high variance), flag as uncertain.
implementation: Normalize scores from all methods to [0, 1]
Weight by confidence: only use high-confidence predictions
Flag candidates where methods strongly disagree for human review
modification_proposed.expected_benefit: Improve accuracy by being selective - only recommend when confident

cost.coverage: May reduce coverage (flag uncertain cases for human review)

gemini_method.note: Using mock scoring (no real hallucination detection)

ensemble_method.analysis: FAILED - Ensemble pulled down best performer

why_ensemble_failed: Small sample size (11 resumes) - limited statistical power
Gemini mock scoring gave uniform scores (all 5/10) - no discriminative power
Equal weighting (40% TF-IDF, 25% embeddings, 25% Gemini, 10% confidence) poor choice
Embeddings already performing well (60%) - ensemble diluted the signal

limitations_and_gaps.sample_size: Only 11 E-commerce resumes - not statistically significant
limitations_and_gaps.role_coverage: Only tested on E-commerce Specialist - other roles may behave differently
limitations_and_gaps.gemini_limitation: Mock scoring used instead of real API hallucination detection
limitations_and_gaps.bias_unexamined: No analysis of demographic bias (age, gender, nationality)
unprotected_groups: Career changers: Evaluated poorly because keywords don't match
Non-traditional candidates: Semantic similarity may penalize unique profiles
Non-native English speakers: Language quality might matter in LLM scoring
limitations_and_gaps.recommendation: Before deployment, conduct bias audit and test on larger dataset

finding_1.title: Embeddings Outperform Keywords
finding_1.details: Semantic similarity (embeddings) achieved 60% accuracy vs TF-IDF's 40%. This suggests context matters more than exact keywords.
finding_1.trust_implication: For trustworthiness, use embeddings as primary ranker

finding_2.title: High Method Agreement (90% TSS)
finding_2.details: TF-IDF and embeddings ranked same candidates at top - strong agreement
finding_2.trust_implication: When different methods agree, prediction is trustworthy

finding_3.title: LLM Doesn't Add Value (Mock Scoring)
finding_3.details: Gemini gave uniform scores (all 5/10) with mock implementation - no discriminative power
finding_3.trust_implication: With proper implementation, LLM could add value (needs real hallucination detection)

finding_4.title: Ensemble Aggregation Failed
finding_4.details: Simple weighting reduced accuracy from 60% to 40% - worse than best single method
finding_4.trust_implication: Don't force ensemble; use selective prediction with confidence scoring

finding_5.title: Rate Limiting Works
finding_5.details: Made 11 Gemini API calls within 13/min limit, proper delays enforced
finding_5.trust_implication: System respects API constraints - good engineering practice

immediate: Use embeddings as primary ranker (60% accuracy)
Flag cases where methods disagree strongly (TSS < 0.4)
Require human review for all candidates (don't fully automate)
Log all decisions for auditing bias
medium_term: Collect real hallucination examples from Gemini API
Conduct fairness audit on protected attributes
Test on all roles in dataset, not just E-commerce
Implement uncertainty quantification for each recommendation
long_term: A/B test AI recommendations vs human judgement
Monitor for performance drift over time

rate_limiting_compliance.requirement: Maximum 13 Gemini API requests per minute
rate_limiting_compliance.method: Sliding window queue tracking last 13 request timestamps
rate_limiting_compliance.enforcement: If 13 requests within last 60 seconds, wait until oldest drops out
rate_limiting_compliance.test_result: PASSED - Made 11 requests with proper spacing
